text.title: Multimodal VUI Interaction Patterns
text.summary: Four multimodal interaction patterns, smart display card layout rules, fallback hierarchy requirements, and timeout standards.

list.item: Voice-initiated, screen-completed: search results, selections (user speaks query; screen shows results; user taps to select)
Screen-initiated, voice-completed: form filling (user sees form; voice fills fields)
Voice + visual feedback: confirmations, progress (voice command → visual confirmation shown + spoken summary)
Voice navigation + visual content: information browsing (voice navigates between visual cards/sections)

rule.statement: Always provide a three-tier fallback hierarchy — primary (voice) → secondary (touch) → tertiary (keyboard if available).
rule.rationale: Users who cannot speak need touch alternatives; users who cannot touch need keyboard. Designing primary-only leaves large user segments unable to complete interactions.

rule.statement: Design the fallback hierarchy before the primary interaction — if touch is broken, voice is broken too for a large user segment.
rule.rationale: Multimodal state sync failures often affect both voice and visual layers simultaneously. The fallback path must work independently of the primary path.

rule.statement: Set visual element timeouts to match displayed content complexity: list (10s), detail card (20s), multi-step form (60s). Minimum 10s for accessibility.
rule.rationale: Agent-suggested timeouts are often too short for elderly users or users with cognitive disabilities. Accessibility-minimum values must be specified explicitly in the prompt.

rule.statement: Spoken summaries must be shorter than visual content — users listening cannot process paragraph-length speech.
rule.rationale: VUI users listening to confirmation speech cannot rewind. Visual content can be re-read; spoken content cannot. Keep summaries to 1–2 sentences maximum.

example.note: Voice input: "Show me recipes for pasta" → Visual: card carousel with 5 recipe images → Spoken: "I found 5 pasta recipes" → User taps to expand, speaks to navigate

text.reference: Generate APL list card JSON for Amazon Echo Show
Haiku prompt: feature brief → multimodal interaction spec with fallback hierarchy
